title: host-asterinas-search: jjf search <query> over bodies/titles/comments
body:
# Goal

Full-text search across issue bodies, titles, and comments.
Beads-style `bd search "<query>"` returning matching issue ids
with snippet context.

# Why now

`host-asterinas-audit` (1588625) identified this as one of two
beads primitives jjforge actually needs to host the 968-issue
asterinas tree queryably. Asterinas's prose-tagged conventions
(Workflow narrative, Status with date stamps, "Found by:"
attribution) live in bodies; without `search`, the daily query
"what tickets discuss DMA coherence?" becomes
`jjf ls --json | jq -r '.[].body' | grep` — works, but doesn't
scale to 968 issues for orchestration.

# Sketch

## CLI

```
jjf search <query> [--status <s>] [--label <l>] [--type <t>] \
                   [--limit N] [--include-comments] [--json]
```

Default: substring match against `title` and `body`. With
`--include-comments`, also matches comment bodies.

## Output

Plain text: one line per match, `id  title  …snippet…`. JSON:
`[{id, title, score, snippet, matched_field}]`. Match snippet
is +/-40 chars around the first hit; `--snippet-context N`
flag tunable.

## Storage

The snapshot cache (commit `69f3043`) already materializes
every body in memory at read time. Add a `search` method on
`Storage` that walks the snapshot and runs case-insensitive
substring match — first pass, no regex, no tokenization, no
ranking beyond match count.

If 968-issue scale shows the substring scan to be slow, add a
naive inverted index (lowercase-word → ids) as a second cache
layer. Defer that until benchmarks show the need.

## Spec bump

Storage spec stays at v2.5 — this is a new query verb, not a
new field or op. Document the new CLI verb in `docs/cli-json.md`.

# Out of scope

- Regex search. Substring is enough for the asterinas migration's
  needs.
- Fuzzy matching / edit distance.
- Ranking-by-relevance (BM25 / TF-IDF). Beads returns
  exact-match-first; we do the same.
- Indexing of memories. They have their own `jjf memories
  <substring>` already.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `jjf search "<query>"` returns issue ids with title and
      snippet, default 20-row limit.
- [ ] `--json` envelope returns `{ok: true, results: [...]}`.
- [ ] `--include-comments` searches comment bodies too.
- [ ] `--status` / `--label` / `--type` filters compose (AND
      semantics with the search query).
- [ ] Subsecond on the 968-issue asterinas tree (the snapshot
      cache makes this trivial).
- [ ] Tests cover empty query, no-match, multi-field hits,
      `--include-comments` toggle.
